name: Config Reference

tasks.title: Write config-reference.md
tasks.body:
Create docs/humans/config-reference.md with every config field documented by section.

Source of truth: the Config struct in internal/config/types.go and the Defaults() function in internal/config/config.go. Read both thoroughly before writing.

Structure the page with a brief intro explaining this is the field-level reference, then one H2 per config section. Sections to cover (in this order): identity, models, pipeline, daemon, logs, retries, failure, git, overlap_advisory, summary, docs, validation, prompts, doctor, unblock, audit, archive, task_classes.

For each section, use an H3 per field. Each field entry must include:
- Type (e.g., string, int, bool, map[string]ModelDef)
- Default value (from Defaults())
- Description of what the field controls
- Example JSON snippet showing a non-default value

For nested structs (ModelDef, PipelineStage, PlanningConfig, etc.), document each nested field within the parent section.

Include a note at the top linking back to the Configuration Guide for context on how tiers and merge semantics work. Add cross-reference links where fields interact (e.g., pipeline.stages model references must exist in the models section).

Do NOT invent fields. Every field must come from the Config struct. If Defaults() doesn't set a value for a field, note the zero-value default.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Config Reference is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify every field in the Config struct (internal/config/types.go) has a corresponding entry in config-reference.md. Diff the struct fields against the documented fields.
Verify default values in the doc match Defaults() in internal/config/config.go

breadcrumbs.text: Wrote complete config-reference.md with all fields from Config struct and Defaults(), organized by section with types, defaults, descriptions, and JSON examples

audit.result_summary: Created docs/humans/config-reference.md, a complete field-level reference for every Wolfcastle configuration option. All 18 config sections are documented with types, default values, descriptions, and JSON examples. Nested structs are documented inline within parent sections. The full default task_classes catalog is presented in categorized tables. Cross-references link to the Configuration Guide for merge semantics and to the Task Classes page for class usage details.

task-0001.objective: Write docs/humans/config-reference.md documenting every Config field by section
task-0001.what_happened: Read internal/config/types.go and internal/config/config.go (Defaults function) as the sole source of truth. Wrote a complete field-level reference organized in the section order specified by the task: identity, models, pipeline, daemon, logs, retries, failure, git, overlap_advisory, summary, docs, validation, prompts, doctor, unblock, audit, archive, task_classes. Each field includes type, default value, description, and a JSON example with a non-default value. Nested structs (ModelDef, PipelineStage, PlanningConfig, ValidationCommand, ClassDef) are documented inline within their parent sections. All default task_classes are listed in tables by category.
went_well: The Config struct and Defaults function provided a clean, complete source of truth with no ambiguity
Nested struct documentation reads naturally inline rather than requiring separate sections
improvements: The research class references a 'light' model that isn't defined in the default models map, worth noting as a potential gap
action_items: Deep-linking pass should add cross-references from other docs pages into specific sections of this reference
